_note: The metal tilde corpus as a .pi program library. For each tilde: an improved spec, the best Anthropic model to EXECUTE it (or none = pure C99 $0), and its .pi card (per-slot tiering). Built by 6 Fable-5 passes over be/docs/commands.md + tildes.json, scored against the ~i LLM-free readiness inspect (metal-desktop r17). Talk back on any card's thread.
source: metal kernel + be/docs/commands.md + joeycastillo.us/metal/tildes.json; scored via be/plans ~i tilde-corpus-as-pi

bands.Hot: Tier 0 — pure C99, $0.00 replay, no model
bands.Warming: Tier 1 — C99 tail, one judgment slot rents the cheapest-capable model
bands.Cold: Tier 2/3 — the model IS the executor

tildes.oneLiner: Mint a project — folder, git on main, be/ scaffold, CLAUDE.md, deploy stub, proposal site, private GitHub repo — then land inside it.
tildes.spec: Parses name/org/FQDN (a dotted arg is the FQDN, required), checks sibling orgs for a name collision per the one-project-one-org rule, and refuses to overwrite an existing folder. Then the chain is pure mechanics: mkdir, git init -b main, scaffold the be/ tree, write CLAUDE.md + deploy.json + proposal/index.html with placeholders substituted, commit, gh repo create --private --push, cd in, print the inline Created summary. The only judgment moment is the org/collision dialogue when args are incomplete — with full args the run is 100% deterministic. No HTML report; recommends ~p next.
tildes.execNote: 90% Hot: with full args every step is a scripted action-unit; the org-pick/collision dialogue is the sole escalation. Corrects tildes.json's “sonnet” — no standing model needed.
tildes.pi:
~new.pi
---
name: new
protocol: [parse_args, sanitize_name, require_fqdn, collide_check_sibling_orgs, refuse_existing_folder, mkdir_root, git_init_main, scaffold_be_tree, write_claude_md, write_deploy_stub, fill_proposal_template, write_index_html, write_board_md, commit_initial, gh_repo_create_private_push, prompt_firestore_setup, cd_project, print_created_summary, log_telemetry]
dor: exempt          # nothing exists yet to pre-read
dod: standard        # gate applies to the committed scaffold
verb: "created as"
model: none          # escalation only: missing-arg dialogue
slots:
  - id: org_collision_dialogue   tier: warm   hint: fires ONLY when org is absent or a sibling-org name collision hits; gh api lists the options, operator picks from a deterministic menu — haiku parses a freeform reply at most. Full args = slot never opens = $0.
schema/data: arg grammar `<name> [org] <fqdn>` — dotted token binds to fqdn; second bare token binds to org.
---
One triple in {name, org, fqdn} → one project out, private, scaffolded, loaded. The tail walks the 19 units in order; any gh failure keeps local state intact.

tildes.oneLiner: Census every project Be can reach — local repos, remote Be repos per org, open transfers — in four inline tables.
tildes.spec: Walks root-level directories classifying Be-managed (has be/) vs plain repos, gathering org/branch/status/last-commit per repo and Last Run from be/telemetry.json tails. Discovers GitHub orgs dynamically from local remotes, runs gh repo list per org, and includes remote repos only if the API confirms a be/ folder — non-Be remotes are noise. Scans be/transfers/ for open handoffs, applies the optional name filter, prints four sections inline, logs telemetry verb “listed in”. DoD-exempt; no artifact, no commit.
tildes.execNote: 100% Hot: a census in a costume; every field is a git/gh/file read formatted into a fixed table. Corrects tildes.json's “haiku” — zero model needed.
tildes.pi:
~li.pi
---
name: li
protocol: [scan_root_dirs, classify_be_vs_repo, gather_git_state_per_repo, read_telemetry_tail, collect_orgs_from_remotes, gh_repo_list_per_org, api_check_remote_be_folder, scan_transfers, apply_name_filter, render_four_section_table, log_telemetry]
dor: exempt
dod: exempt          # utility, inline only
verb: "listed in"
model: none
slots: []
schema/data: sections = [LOCAL_BE, LOCAL_REPOS, REMOTE_BE, OPEN_TRANSFERS]; row = {name, path, org, branch, status, last_commit, last_run}; transfers show "(none)" rather than vanish.
---
A directory walk plus gh calls folded into four fixed-width tables. Nothing decides; everything reads.

tildes.oneLiner: Resolve the target, clone if absent, pull only if clean, cd in, recommend the next board item, and pop the live board.
tildes.spec: Resolves URL/path/short-name to C:\<repo-name>, clones if missing, health-checks (status, diff --stat, log -1, board presence), and pulls only on a clean tree — never over dirty work, never pushes. Switches the working directory so every later tilde operates on that project, computes a Next recommendation using ~b's selection walk without moving anything, and prints the inline Loaded summary. Then open-board.bat pops the most-recent live board view in the DEFAULT browser, and presence.exe check-then-stamp makes cross-session collisions visible, advisory and non-fatal. DoD-exempt; inline only.
tildes.execNote: 100% Hot, already shipped Tier-0 in metal-de r257: resolution, sync guards, Next-item walk, board pop, and presence are all rule-table mechanics. Corrects tildes.json's “haiku” — no model in the loop.
tildes.pi:
~l.pi
---
name: l
protocol: [resolve_target, clone_if_absent, health_check_git, check_board_presence, pull_if_clean_else_report, cd_repo, walk_boards_skip_flagged, take_top_ready_item, map_verb_to_tilde, print_loaded_summary, open_board_view_default_browser, presence_check_warn, presence_stamp]
dor: exempt
dod: exempt          # utility, inline only
verb: "loaded in"
model: none
slots: []
schema/data: verb-map {add to→~a, update with→~u, fix in→~f, plan tests for→~pt, test across→~t, designed for→~d}; skip-flags [CANCELLED, SUPERSEDED, SHELVED, blocked]; presence keys off the declared project, not cwd.
---
One name in → context switched, state reported, Next recommended, board live on screen. The Next line is a recommendation; ~b pulls it.

tildes.oneLiner: Deep-read a repo, URL, or folder and publish what it is, how it's built, what patterns it carries, and what's still unanswered.
tildes.spec: Reads the target end-to-end, then synthesizes an inspect report — What It Is, Structure, Stack, Patterns Found, Open Questions; architecture targets use the Intent → Purpose → What frame — rendered to be/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.html. The tail is deterministic: write the HTML, update the index, commit, push, log telemetry “inspected in”, pop the report. Read-only against source — its own artifact is the only write. DoD gate applies; production-verify is typically N/A for a discovery tilde.
tildes.execNote: Cold-lean: the whole tilde IS the deep-read-plus-synthesis judgment; only the render/commit tail is mechanical. Matches tildes.json — no correction.
tildes.pi:
~i.pi
---
name: i
protocol: [read_target_full, render_report_html, update_index, commit, push, log_telemetry, open_report]
dor: standard        # 4-read pre-flight incl. a-d-d if present
dod: standard        # production-verify typically N/A
verb: "inspected in"
model: fable         # the model IS the executor here
slots:
  - id: deep_read_synthesis   tier: cold   hint: fable reads the whole target and fills the five sections (Intent→Purpose→What for architecture); Open Questions is where board work gets born — anchored by the report template, verified read-only.
schema/data: sections = [What It Is, Structure, Stack, Patterns Found, Open Questions].
---
Read everything, change nothing, say what's true. The five-section frame is the grammar; the judgment fills it.

tildes.oneLiner: Audit the loaded project for loose ends, resolve each with operator approval, ship the status report, and cd back home.
tildes.spec: The inverse of ~l: identifies the loaded project, audits git status/diff/unpushed commits, In Progress board items, and deploy-vs-commit state, then presents each unclean finding with fixed resolution options — commit/stash/leave, push/leave, move-back/leave — never auto-committing, pushing, or discarding. Always renders be/builds/YYYY-MM-DD-unload-<project>.html (Project Summary, Git State, Board State, Deploy State, Session Work) into the PROJECT's be/builds/, updates the index, commits + pushes, then cd's back to the calling directory with the inline Unloaded summary. The audit and menus are deterministic; the operator supplies the decisions. DoD gate applies.
tildes.execNote: 92% Hot: audit, menus, report, and context switch are scripted; the only slot is Session-Work summary prose, degradable to a raw commit list at $0. Corrects tildes.json's “sonnet” — no standing model needed.
tildes.pi:
~ul.pi
---
name: ul
protocol: [identify_loaded_project, audit_git_status, audit_diff_stat, audit_unpushed_commits, audit_board_inprogress, audit_deploy_state, present_resolution_menus, apply_operator_choices, render_unload_report, update_index, commit, push, cd_back_calling_dir, print_unloaded_summary]
dor: exempt          # closing out, not opening work
dod: standard        # report lands in the PROJECT's be/builds/
verb: "unloaded from"
model: none          # escalation only: summary prose
slots:
  - id: session_summary_prose   tier: warm   hint: haiku turns since-session commits into two readable lines for the Session Work section; degrade path = verbatim `git log --oneline` list, $0. Everything else in the card is tier-0.
schema/data: resolution menus {uncommitted: [commit, stash, leave], unpushed: [push, leave], in_progress: [move_to_ready, leave]}; report sections = [Project Summary, Git State, Board State, Deploy State, Session Work].
---
Nothing leaves dirty without the operator saying so; the report always ships, clean or not. Load brings a project in — this closes it out.

tildes.oneLiner: Find the real need, mine a-d-d/nous/our apps/prior art before inventing, name every part literally, and define the one smallest move that proves the design.
tildes.spec: A six-section spine sized to the work — Need, Raw User Stories, Sourced Solution, Naming, Shape, First Move — with three optional tools (Choices+Consequences, Escape Routes, Pure Metal Test) pulled in only when the design is load-bearing. The mechanical core is fixed: mine in strict order (kastil-systems/a-d-d → nous C99 primitives + src/sdk → our apps → outside), copy _template.html, render to be/designs/, index/commit/push under the DoD gate. The judgment moments are exactly three — the need under the subject, the minimum viable shape, and the First Move that proves it. Writes no code, no plan, no board items; NOT a delete command.
tildes.execNote: 40% Cold; need/shape/first-move are the deepest judgment moments in the corpus and the model IS the executor. Confirms tildes.json's fable; the source-mining walk and template chain are still C99-hot around it.
tildes.pi:
~design.pi
---
name: design
protocol: [route(subject), preflight(dor-4read), mine(a-d-d, nous, src/sdk, apps, outside — ordered, log every source hit), collision-check(names vs command grid + conventions), fill(_template.html six-spine), render(be/designs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-design-<slug>.html), index, telemetry(verb=designed for), commit, push, dod, latchkey_publish, pop]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "designed for"
model: fable
slots:
  - id: need          tier: cold   hint: who hurts, what's missing, why now — never a restatement of the subject
  - id: raw_stories   tier: warm   hint: unpolished As-a/I-want/So-that seeds for ~p/~e; don't over-write
  - id: sourced_pick  tier: warm   hint: the SEARCH is deterministic (ordered mine, sources named); only the pick among candidates is judgment — reuse beats invention
  - id: naming        tier: warm   hint: literal names only; metaphor must also be the accurate word; grid collision-check is hot
  - id: shape         tier: cold   hint: minimum viable structure — parts, boundaries, explicit out-of-scope
  - id: first_move    tier: cold   hint: the single smallest commit that, if it works, proves the design correct
  - id: tools_gate    tier: warm   hint: include Choices/Escape-Routes/Pure-Metal only when stakes earn them — ceremony check, not checklist
---
Six spine sections always; three tools only when load-bearing. Read-only except its own artifact.

tildes.oneLiner: Turn six intent axes into a full UI design system — constraint-resolved rules, component behavior, and a ready-to-use CSS variables block — with zero named styles.
tildes.spec: Collects or infers 6 axes (Emotion, Density, Reality, Hierarchy, Rhythm, Expression), runs the required constraint layer (detect contradictions, keep the top 2 traits, resolve toward coherence), and maps everything onto exactly 5 paradigms — never a named external style. The deterministic core is a finite rule engine: 5×3×3×3×4×3 = 1,620 axis combinations, each resolving to spacing/type/color/depth tables and a :root CSS block; the only judgment is inferring unstated axes from the subject. Renders the 4-section report from _laf-template.html to be/designs/, DoD gate applies, and the review flags every inferred axis. Produces a system, not CSS files or code.
tildes.execNote: 70% Warming trending Hot: the 1,620-cell constraint engine and output tables are pure C99; the sole slot is axis classification, a light-classify job. Corrects tildes.json's fable — renting deep judgment for a table lookup plus a classifier is the exact waste the scoring exists to catch. Explicit-axes invocations bypass the slot entirely = $0.
tildes.pi:
~laf.pi
---
name: laf
protocol: [route(subject+hints), parse(explicit axes from args), infer(missing axes — slot), constrain(detect contradictions, rank top-2, resolve per coherence table), resolve(1620-cell rule engine → spacing/type/color/depth/density), emit(4 sections + :root CSS block), fill(_laf-template.html), render(be/designs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM-laf-<slug>.html), index, telemetry(verb=styled for), commit, push, dod, latchkey_publish, pop, review(flag inferred axes)]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "styled for"
model: haiku
slots:
  - id: axis_inference  tier: warm   hint: classify subject words → 6 axis values ("calm dense dashboard" → emotion=calm, density=dense, infer rest); explicit args skip it = $0
schema/data: axes{emotion:5, density:3, reality:3, hierarchy:3, rhythm:4, expression:3} · paradigms[representational, abstract, hybrid, structural, emotional] · coherence table (dense/expressive/calm/premium/aggressive rules)
---
Modifiers (~laf style|density|motion <v>) re-enter at constrain() with one axis swapped — new artifact, same engine.

tildes.oneLiner: Write the plan as a v1 hypothesis — intent, scope, approach, phases, constraints, named Unknowns, and the signal that triggers v2.
tildes.spec: Renders a plan to be/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>[-vN].html with seven required sections; Unknowns and When-To-Revisit are mandatory per Rule 6 — an empty Unknowns section is suspect, and revision is the default, not the exception. Versioning is mechanical: v2 gets a -v2- filename plus a Supersedes section, v1 is marked SUPERSEDED in the index, and load_master_plan() follows the newest v by mtime automatically. Sets “Plan — Awaiting Approval”, indexes, commits, pushes under the DoD gate. Planning IS the review — one step, one artifact.
tildes.execNote: 30% Cold; the Unknowns slot is floor=cold FOREVER (naming what you don't know is irreducible judgment) and the approach is real synthesis. Confirms tildes.json's fable; only the render/version/commit tail goes C99.
tildes.pi:
~plan.pi
---
name: plan
protocol: [route(thing, goal), preflight(dor-4read), version-check(existing plan for subject → -vN- filename + Supersedes section + mark v1 SUPERSEDED in index), fill(7 required sections in order), banner(Plan — Awaiting Approval), render(be/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>[-vN].html), index, telemetry(verb=planned for), commit, push, dod, latchkey_publish, pop]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "planned for"
model: fable
slots:
  - id: intent_scope   tier: warm   hint: the why + in/out boundary — anchored by the operator's ask
  - id: approach       tier: cold   hint: the how; architecture plans restructure as Intent → Purpose → What
  - id: phases         tier: warm   hint: sequenced delivery — feeds ~e's decomposition
  - id: unknowns       tier: cold   floor: cold FOREVER — naming what you don't know cannot be replayed; empty = you haven't thought hard enough
  - id: revisit_trigger tier: warm  hint: the concrete v2 signal — a learning, milestone, failure mode, or check-in
---
Plans are hypotheses, not contracts; a plan that survives a whole project unrevised is suspect.

tildes.oneLiner: Decompose a plan into {what, verb, context} triples sized 3 or 5, packed into 13–21-point boards ordered by value then unblocking.
tildes.spec: Reads a ~p output and produces be/board.md: every item is a triple estimated at exactly 3 or 5 (smaller → recompose, larger → decompose; the anchor is “a 5 is one college-student night, complete and perfect”), boards total 13–21 points, ordered by value then dependency removal. The infra-awareness rule is deterministic — any item touching a new host/port/service auto-emits a sibling firewall/verify item. The validation, packing, and sibling-emission are mechanical; the judgment is the decomposition itself and the value ranking. Renders the build-plan report to be/plans/, writes board.md alongside, DoD gate applies.
tildes.execNote: 55% Warming; structured decomposition into triples is the slot and sonnet is the cheapest-capable model for it. Confirms tildes.json's sonnet. Sizing back-loads: as telemetry accumulates duration truth per verb/context, the 3-vs-5 call becomes a C99 table lookup.
tildes.pi:
~estimate.pi
---
name: estimate
protocol: [route(plan), read(be/plans latest or named), decompose(slot), validate(every estimate ∈ {3,5} — reject/recompose loop), rank(value-first), pack(boards 13 ≤ Σpts ≤ 21), order(boards by value, then dependency removal), infra-scan(new host|port|service → emit sibling firewall/verify item), write(be/board.md), render(be/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.html — Boards Overview, Board Details, Dependencies, Value Rationale), index, telemetry(verb=estimated for), commit(board.md alongside), push, dod, latchkey_publish, pop]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "estimated for"
model: sonnet
slots:
  - id: decompose   tier: warm   hint: plan phases → {what, verb, context} triples; verb must map to a work tilde (~a/~u/~f/~t)
  - id: size        tier: warm   hint: 3 or 5 only, anchored to the college-student-night rule; graduates hot as telemetry duration truth back-fills the table
  - id: value_rank  tier: warm   hint: greatest value first — the operator's stated goal is the anchor
schema/data: fib={3,5} · board_pts=[13..21] · infra_triggers=[new host, new port, new service]
---
Ports don't open themselves: every deploy-to-new-host item ships with its firewall sibling, mechanically.

tildes.oneLiner: Frame the work as As-a/I-want/So-that with acceptance criteria testable enough to hand straight to ~t.
tildes.spec: Plans one piece of work as a user story and renders to be/plans/ with four sections: User Story, Acceptance Criteria, Scope, Approach — then reviews the story immediately before the chain continues. The story scaffold is fixed grammar; the judgment is writing acceptance criteria as verifiable oracles, which shares the TLT library so each criterion lands as a runnable check, not prose. The lightweight sibling of ~p: story framing instead of the seven-section spine. DoD gate applies; index, commit, push.
tildes.execNote: 45% Warming; acceptance-criteria drafting is structured decomposition (each criterion must be a binary-checkable oracle), sonnet is cheapest-capable. Confirms tildes.json's sonnet; the As-a/I-want/So-that frame itself is grammar the tail can hold.
tildes.pi:
~plan-user.pi
---
name: plan-user
protocol: [route(story ask), preflight(dor-4read), frame(As a <who>, I want <what>, So that <why>), criteria(slot — each criterion an oracle, TLT-library shaped), scope+approach, review(validate the story before moving on), render(be/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.html — User Story, Acceptance Criteria, Scope, Approach), index, telemetry(verb=planned for), commit, push, dod, latchkey_publish, pop]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "planned for"
model: sonnet
slots:
  - id: story_frame  tier: warm   hint: fit the ask into the fixed grammar; who/what/why extraction, anchored by the operator's words
  - id: criteria     tier: warm   hint: testable oracles only — write each as a check the TLT library can run; "user can X" must resolve to a pass/fail probe
---
The story is the spec; the criteria are the test plan wearing story clothes.

tildes.oneLiner: Define what to test before any test is built — cases, edge cases, and dependencies for a feature or area.
tildes.spec: Plans the test strategy and renders to be/plans/ with four sections: What to Test, Test Cases, Edge Cases, Dependencies — reviewed immediately, before ~bt/~t build anything. Upstream of test construction by design: it defines coverage, not code. The functional-case enumeration follows the target's surface mechanically; the judgment is edge imagination — the failure modes nothing in the spec names. DoD gate applies; index, commit, push.
tildes.execNote: 40% Cold today: edge imagination is deep adversarial judgment, so the model IS the executor. Corrects tildes.json's sonnet — under-renting the one slot that exists to see what the spec didn't. Cold→Hot by design: every novel edge graduates into an edge-rule library, so replay against known shapes trends to $0.
tildes.pi:
~plan-test.pi
---
name: plan-test
protocol: [route(target), preflight(dor-4read), enumerate(functional cases from the target's surface — inputs, states, outputs), edge-replay(match target shape against edge-rule library — known edges are $0), edge-imagine(slot — only for shapes the library doesn't cover), map(dependencies), review(validate strategy before tests are built), render(be/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<slug>.html — What to Test, Test Cases, Edge Cases, Dependencies), index, telemetry(verb=plan tests for), commit, push, dod, latchkey_publish, pop, graduate(novel edges → edge-rule library)]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "plan tests for"
model: fable
slots:
  - id: edge_imagine  tier: cold   hint: the failure modes the spec never named — adversarial imagination; each novel edge graduates to a replayable rule (Cold→Hot)
  - id: case_select   tier: warm   hint: which functional cases matter most — smoke → functional → integration → destructive ordering anchors it
---
The library eats the cold slot one edge at a time; the tilde gets cheaper every time it runs.

tildes.oneLiner: The kASTIL pattern library — list, show, and apply are free deterministic reads; extract lifts a new pattern from real work for the operator to name and bless.
tildes.spec: Routes four verbs against kastil-systems/a-d-d/patterns/ (PATTERNS.md catalog, PRACTICES.md, _TEMPLATE.md). Bare/<name>/apply are DoD-exempt reads: list the catalog, show a doc byte-verbatim, or render a pattern's Structure as a checklist against current work then implement to it — natural language like “make it a slot” resolves here case/spacing-insensitively, including aka names. Extract is the one gated verb: lift a reusable pattern from the current build, strip the application, fill the 8-part template, propose 2–4 true names — the operator picks (Rule 10). Patterns are discovered by building, then named — never invented in the abstract. Consult the catalog before ~d/~b so known patterns get applied, not re-invented.
tildes.execNote: 95% Hot: catalog resolve, byte-verbatim show, and checklist render are pure C99 reads, $0. Corrects tildes.json's blanket fable: only the extract sub-verb (scored 25, floor=cold FOREVER) escalates and rents fable; apply's implement step rents the work tilde's own model, not this card's.
tildes.pi:
~patterns.pi
---
name: patterns
protocol: [route(verb: bare|<name>|apply|extract), read(PATTERNS.md catalog), resolve(name — case/spacing-insensitive, aka-aware), show(pattern doc byte-verbatim) | checklist(render Structure vs current work → hand implement to the active work tilde) | extract-path, on-extract: [read(current design/build context), abstract(slot), propose-names(2–4 true names, slot), WAIT(operator blesses the name — hard stop), write(patterns/<name>.md from _TEMPLATE.md), append(PATTERNS.md catalog row), graduate(new always-do rule → PRACTICES.md), render, index, telemetry(verb=patterned in), commit, push, dod, latchkey_publish, pop]]
dor: class (extract) | exempt (bare, <name>, apply)
dod: class (extract) | exempt (bare, <name>, apply)
verb: "patterned in"
model: fable
slots:
  - id: abstract       tier: cold   floor: cold FOREVER — strip the one product out of the work until only the reusable shape remains; Problem·Forces·Solution·Structure·Consequences·Known-Uses·Related·Anti-patterns
  - id: propose_names  tier: cold   hint: 2–4 evocative TRUE names, kASTIL house style; operator picks — never auto-committed
schema/data: catalog=kastil-systems/a-d-d/patterns/PATTERNS.md · current=[Trust, Slot] · template=_TEMPLATE.md (8 parts)
---
Read verbs cost nothing and never will; extract is the library's only way to grow, and it always ends at the operator's blessing.

tildes.oneLiner: Add a new capability to the project, review it, and ship the full report chain — paired with ~t before it counts.
tildes.spec: Plans the change set and writes the new code, then reviews immediately before the chain continues. Renders a build report to be/builds/ (What Was Added, Decisions Made, Files Changed, What's Next), updates the index, commits, pushes, launches the demo, opens the report — DoD gate on all of it. The auth-gate rule is deterministic: new web project + deploy.json auth.enabled → copy the three auth-gate files and wire the overlay, no judgment involved. Ships as ~a~t per Rule 7 — the add isn't done until the test half of the loop is green.
tildes.execNote: Warming (70%): the tail is C99, but the change-set planning + code-gen slot is real code writing, which rents opus (cheapest-capable for edit/gen). Matches tildes.json; opus fills two slots, not the whole tilde.
tildes.pi:
~add.pi
---
name: add
protocol: [route, dor-preflight, board:ready→wip, auth-gate-check(deploy.json), apply-change-set, review, board:wip→done, render:be/builds, index-update, telemetry, commit+push, latchkey-publish, dod-gate, demo-launch, pop-report, chain:~t]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "add to"
model: opus
slots:
  - id: change_set_plan   tier: warm   hint: decompose <what> into files+edits; anchored by the board triple's context field
  - id: code_gen          tier: warm   hint: write the new source; review step + the paired ~t flip are the oracle
schema/data: auth-gate rule table — {trigger: new web project ∧ auth.enabled, action: copy 3 auth-gate files + overlay div} — fully deterministic, lives in protocol not slots
---
{<what>, add to, <context>} → change-set → source → proof. The tail is fixed; only what-to-write is rented.

tildes.oneLiner: Change existing code to the stated target, prove before/after, and run the full report chain — paired with ~t.
tildes.spec: Grounds the request against the code that exists (the judgment moment — finding the true target of “the nav bar”), applies the edit, and reviews immediately. Renders a build report to be/builds/ (What Was Updated, Before/After, Files Changed, What's Next), updates the index, commits, pushes, launches the demo, opens the report — DoD gate applies. Before/After is the section that makes ~u distinct from ~a: the report must show both states. Ships as ~u~t per Rule 7.
tildes.execNote: Warming (70%): target-grounding + edit is code work; opus is the cheapest-capable model for edits. Grounding back-loads toward Hot as telemetry accumulates real target→file mappings, but today the slot is rented. Matches tildes.json.
tildes.pi:
~update.pi
---
name: update
protocol: [route, dor-preflight, board:ready→wip, snapshot-before, apply-edit, review, board:wip→done, render:be/builds(before/after), index-update, telemetry, commit+push, latchkey-publish, dod-gate, demo-launch, pop-report, chain:~t]

dod: class

  - id: target_grounding   tier: warm   hint: resolve <what> to actual files/symbols; back-loads Hot as telemetry maps names→paths

{<what>, update with, <context>} → ground → diff → proof. Before/After is captured mechanically; only where-and-how is rented.

tildes.oneLiner: Find the root cause, fix it, and ship a test that fails before and passes after — the flip is the proof.
tildes.spec: Diagnoses the bug, fixes it, and writes a regression test that reproduces it (fails before, passes after); if no testable surface exists, the report documents manual verification instead. Reviews the fix, then renders a build report to be/builds/ (What Was Broken, Root Cause, What Was Fixed, Test Added, Files Changed, What's Next) and runs the standard tail: index, commit, push, demo, report, DoD gate. The fail→pass flip is a binary machine oracle — the one work tilde whose judgment self-verifies at $0. Every fix ships with proof; no proof, no done.
tildes.execNote: Warming (60%): diagnosis + fix is code work rented to opus, but the test flip machine-verifies the output — the model proposes, the flip disposes. Matches tildes.json; the oracle is Tier 0, only the diagnosis is rented.

~fix.pi
---
name: fix
protocol: [route, dor-preflight, board:ready→wip, run-test:expect-fail, apply-fix, run-test:expect-pass, review, board:wip→done, render:be/builds(root-cause), index-update, telemetry, commit+push, latchkey-publish, dod-gate, demo-launch, pop-report]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "fix in"
model: opus
slots:
  - id: diagnosis   tier: warm   hint: name the root cause; machine-verifiable — the fail→pass flip is the $0 oracle
  - id: fix_edit    tier: warm   hint: minimal change that flips the test; expect-fail/expect-pass gates bound it deterministically
schema/data: fallback — no test surface → report carries manual-verification steps in Test Added; flip gates skip, review carries the proof burden
---
red → cause → change → green. The flip brackets the judgment; everything outside it is fixed tail.

tildes.oneLiner: Orchestrate the board — pull every Ready item in order, dispatch its mapped tilde, checkpoint per item, one report at the end.
tildes.spec: With an argument, builds the named thing directly. Bare ~b is pure orchestration: read board.md, take the first unblocked board, and for each Ready item move it to In Progress, dispatch its mapped tilde (~a/~u/~f/~t), review, move to Done, and commit+push — each item's push is a crash checkpoint, never hold completed work uncommitted. Demo and report fire ONCE at board end, then the next board unblocks. Preflight, tarball-audit, and auth-gate rules are deterministic checks, not judgment. The loop itself makes zero decisions; it rents its inner tildes' judgment.
tildes.execNote: Hot (85%): the orchestrator is a deterministic read-dispatch-checkpoint loop in C99; all judgment lives in the inner tildes it dispatches. Corrects tildes.json's opus — opus belongs to the inner ~a/~u/~f work, not to ~b itself.

~build.pi

name: build
protocol: [route, read:board.md, select:first-unblocked, foreach-ready:[board:ready→wip, preflight(remote-prereqs), dispatch:<item.cmd>, review, board:wip→done, render-board-view, commit+push], unblock-next-board, render:be/builds(one-report), index-update, telemetry, dod-gate, demo-launch:once, pop-report+board-view]

---
board in → items out, atomically. The loop is a program counter over triples; judgment is rented downstream, never here.

tildes.oneLiner: Design and write the test suite for an area — smoke first, destructive last — and ship the coverage report.
tildes.spec: Writes the actual tests for the target, runs them, and reviews coverage and correctness before the chain continues. Renders a test report to be/tests/ (Tests Built, Coverage, Test Results, Remaining Gaps) then the standard tail: index, commit, push, demo, report, DoD gate. The ordering rule is deterministic: smoke → functional → integration/E2E → destructive; critical path first, budget-exhausting last. Coverage design is the judgment moment — unlike ~f there is no self-verifying flip, so review and Remaining Gaps carry the proof burden.
tildes.execNote: Warming (55%): coverage design + test code-gen has no binary oracle to anchor it, so the slot rents opus (code-gen); the ordering, run, and report tail are C99. Matches tildes.json; only the design slot is rented — execution and ordering are $0.

<area> → ordered suite → run → gaps named. The order is law; only what-to-cover is rented.

tildes.oneLiner: Run the tests against the work, fix what breaks until green, and ship the result — the verify half of every work loop.
tildes.spec: Tests the current work or a named target in the fixed order (smoke → functional → integration/E2E → destructive), fixes failures inline and re-tests until green — or files a ~f when scope grows. Renders a test report to be/tests/ (What Was Tested, Results, Fixes Applied, Remaining Issues) then the standard tail with DoD gate. It is the closing half of every Rule-7 pair (~a~t, ~u~t, ~f~t, ~b~t): no work tilde is done until ~t is green. Binary oracles run at $0; judgment appears only in selecting the plan and reading non-binary results.
tildes.execNote: Warming (75%): plan-select is structured selection, not code-gen — sonnet is cheapest-capable; binary oracles replay at $0 and the fix path escalates to ~f/opus only on red. Corrects tildes.json's opus — opus is the escalation, not the executor.

tildes.oneLiner: Poll JR for the next winner, dispatch its tilde, ship, repeat — until JR says hide.
tildes.spec: Authenticates with the operator's mesh session token, GETs the-jack-rabbit.net/p/<project>/next.json, and loops: each winner {what,verb,context,cmd,pts} lands Ready on board.md, its mapped tilde executes (own artifact + commit + push + Signal), JR catches the Signal and re-decides, repeat until {status:hide}. Termination is JR's call — no --max flag; winners bill per iteration, hides don't; one chase per project per session (second gets 409). Failure modes are mechanical: 60s backoff then fall back to bare ~b, auth-refresh mid-loop, no resume on crash. No HTML artifact of its own — an inline summary with ledger total and artifact links; the DoD gate belongs to the inner tildes.
tildes.execNote: Hot (100%): pure protocol loop (auth/GET/dispatch/backoff) in C99; every judgment belongs to the inner tilde JR selects. Corrects tildes.json's sonnet — the loop needs no model at all.
tildes.pi:
~chase.pi

dor: exempt
dod: exempt
verb: "chased for"
model: none
slots: []
schema/data: JR contract — winner:{what,verb,context,cmd,pts} | hide:{status:hide}; pricing $0.0004×4 primes + $0.0002×2 relays per winner, hide=free; 409 on concurrent chase per project
---
GET → dispatch → Signal → GET. A while-loop with a bank account; JR decides, the inner tildes think, this card just runs.

tildes.oneLiner: Commit, push, deploy, and prove the ship landed — report, site sync, telemetry, all chain-final gates in one shot.
tildes.spec: Chain-final: stage → commit (no co-author lines, ever) → push → render the ship report to be/ships/ (What Shipped, Version, Commits Included, Deploy Target) → update the index. Then the sync sweep: joeycastillo.us/metal's four pages if present, ~h display vs current commands, revision footers to the post-commit number, and Mercury telemetry push/pull (non-fatal — a ship never fails on the hub). When deploy.json exists the deploy chain runs and the live target must serve the new bytes — Done means externally verified, not pushed. Everything here is mechanical except two moments: the ship-summary prose and the architecture-page sync.
tildes.execNote: 85% Hot; the stage/commit/push/render/verify/sync chain is pure protocol, and the two prose slots (ship summary, arch-page sync) rent haiku only when invoked — degradable to a commit list at $0. Corrects tildes.json's sonnet: no orchestrator needed to execute the tail.

tildes.oneLiner: Turn the latest ship report into a 60–90 second spoken avatar video for people who watch but don't read.
tildes.spec: Reads the most recent be/ships/*.html (falls back to the latest build report), extracts what shipped, and writes a 150–200 word script to be/briefs/ — lead with what changed, name specifics, close with what's next, tone 2 for team / tone 4 for external. Then the HeyGen jacket: POST v2/video/generate with HEYGEN_API_KEY, poll video_status until completed, download the 1080p mp4 beside the script; no key = save script and prompt (Joey pastes, Be installs via setx). Renders a brief report to be/builds/ with an embedded <video>, commits, pushes. The script IS the artifact — DoD passes on it alone; the video is a bonus.
tildes.execNote: 60% Warming; the only judgment is 150–200 words of calibrated light prose, squarely haiku's band. The HeyGen call is a deterministic C# foreign-API jacket, $0 judgment. Corrects tildes.json's sonnet: no decomposition here, just a short script.

tildes.oneLiner: Prove the deploy is alive from the right vantage — green is free, red gets the Rule-13 diagnosis ladder.
tildes.spec: Probes the deploy target with the correct protocol from the correct plane — never a single curl from the dev box, whose router makes 000 a vantage failure, not a verdict. Green path is pure protocol: resolve the real endpoint (DNS/proxy/Worker first), match wire-over-TLS vs HTTP, expect the payload, report live. Red path climbs the Rule-13 ladder — same-plane probe, protocol match, process + recent logs (a LISTENing port with days-old logs is hung, not firewalled), name/proxy check — and names the cause with evidence or says “I don't know yet.” No HTML artifact; the finding reports inline.
tildes.execNote: 95% Hot; the green path is a deterministic probe sequence at $0. The ONLY escalation is red-path diagnosis (scores 40, Cold), which rents fable for the evidence-ladder judgment. Corrects tildes.json's fable: fable is the exception slot, not the executor — a healthy deploy costs $0.00.

~monitor.pi

schema/data: verdicts = [live, hung, unreachable-from-vantage, wrong-protocol, dns-missing, unknown]; hung-test = port LISTEN + stale journalctl
---

tildes.oneLiner: Refactor the named code, verify it immediately, and mine every “could be better” into the Kaizen board.
tildes.spec: Does the refactor/cleanup on the specified code, reviews correctness immediately (the review is part of the tilde, not a follow-up), then renders a report to be/retros/ — What Was Changed, Before/After, Decisions Made, Impact, What Could Be Better. Every What-Could-Be-Better bullet becomes one Board K Kaizen item with a source:<retro-filename> annotation, no points, opt-in only. Index update, commit with board.md alongside, push, demo launch, report open. The refactor payload is the deep judgment; the surrounding chain is mechanical.
tildes.execNote: 35% Cold; the model IS the executor for the refactor payload (code-heavy restructure + immediate correctness review). Matches tildes.json opus — the one Ship tilde where the current field is already right. Goes Hot last.

tildes.oneLiner: Read everything since the last retro — artifacts and debug logs — and turn what it learned into Kaizen items.
tildes.spec: Chain-final: gathers all Be artifacts not yet retro'd (never re-retros old ground) and greps the latest be/debug/ logs for tool failures, permission rejections, retries, and slow ops — that scan is mechanical, pattern-matching, $0. Renders be/retros/YYYY-MM-DD-retro.html (What Happened, What Went Well, What Could Be Better, Tool Failures, Permission Issues, Performance Notes, Commitments) and appends one Board K Kaizen item per improvement point, each traceable via source: annotation. Commits board.md with the retro, pushes, fires Mercury sync (non-fatal). The synthesis — what the evidence actually means, and what to commit to — is the one cold moment.
tildes.execNote: 55% Cold-bound: cross-artifact synthesis into Went-Well/Could-Be-Better/Commitments is a dedicated-reasoner binding, while the gather/scan/kaizen/commit tail is pure C99. Matches tildes.json fable, now scoped to the synthesis slot only.

tildes.oneLiner: Rebuild and redeploy <project>.dev from board and build state — fires at board completion, not every commit.
tildes.spec: Board-completion-triggered regen of the developer surface (Rule 5's third zone): read the just-completed board's Done items plus the next Ready board, collect WIP artifacts (build reports, screenshots, test output), run be/tools/build_dev_surface.py (stdlib-only), deploy via wrangler pages deploy with CLOUDFLARE_DNS_TOKEN_DEV, log telemetry with verb “refreshed in.” Opt-in: requires three registered zones and a dev subdomain in deploy.json — no dev zone, no fire. Explicitly NOT part of ~s; it converges the dev surface between ships.
tildes.execNote: 98% Hot; trigger detection, artifact collection, generator run, and wrangler deploy are a fully deterministic $0 replay with zero judgment slots. Corrects tildes.json's haiku: no model needed at all — the cleanest Tier-0 tilde in Ship.

tildes.oneLiner: Append a {subject, tagged as, tag} triple to be/tags.json, rebuild the viewer, commit, push — anything becomes searchable.
tildes.spec: A DoD-exempt utility with four verbs: attach (subject + tag + optional quoted context), bare view (grouped by subject, newest first), search (case-insensitive substring across subject/tag/date/context), and rm by ID. Every write auto-increments a never-reused ID, stamps author from git config and date/time from the clock, appends the entry, rebuilds be/tags.html from inline JSON, commits both files, and pushes. Telemetry logs verb “tagged in.” Flat strings only, no frameworks — the triple IS the data.
tildes.execNote: 100% Hot (Tier 0): parse-append-render-commit is a fixed protocol with zero judgment; corrects tildes.json's haiku — no model is needed at all.

tildes.oneLiner: Get, set, or reset Be runtime settings in be/config.json — every write validates against the schema, commits, and pushes.
tildes.spec: A DoD-exempt utility over a versioned flat JSON with dot-notation namespaces. Bare shows all keys as key/current/default/description; a key shows one; key + value sets; --reset restores defaults. Three known keys — reports.display (preview/print/silent), reports.chain_timing (each/final), remote_control.enabled (true/false, read by launchers on next go). Missing file means all defaults; invalid key or value errors inline with the valid set and writes nothing; every write commits + pushes so config travels with the repo. No artifact, no telemetry.
tildes.execNote: 100% Hot (Tier 0): schema-validated JSON get/set with a closed key table; corrects tildes.json's haiku — the exact case Joey asked about, $0.00 deterministic replay.

tildes.oneLiner: Reconstruct the last unfinished session — prompt log first, git second — and resume the broken chain from exactly where it stopped.
tildes.spec: A DoD-exempt recovery utility. Step 0 reads the centralized prompt log (be/prompts.jsonl) filtered to this hostname to find the candidate session and its cwd — intent before evidence. Steps 1–3 are table-driven: dirty-file locations fingerprint which tilde ran, and a seven-step chain map (work→review→report→index→commit→push→launch) names the break point. Step 4 resumes from that point without redoing completed steps; Step 5 renders the recovery report to be/builds/. Never pulls, never discards, never silently switches cwd; multi-item recovery commits atomically per item with a report per item.
tildes.execNote: Warming (60): the fingerprint and break-point tables are Tier-0 C99, but reading a raw diff + last-5 prompts to name intent when the tables are ambiguous is a structured-read slot; corrects tildes.json's opus — the resumed work rents the inner tilde's own model, so ~cr itself never needs code-gen weight.

tildes.oneLiner: When the AI's judgment is broken, halt, grade the blast radius, let the user decide every rollback, brief the next session, and kill this one.
tildes.spec: The 7-step trust-recovery protocol for a pattern of bad work, not a single bug: HALT, BLAST RADIUS from the last known-good ~s/~r commit, read-only AUDIT grading every change verified/suspect/broken, ROLLBACK where the AI proposes and the user disposes, HOUSEKEEPING until the repo and board are clean and accurate, LOBOTOMY — write the WTF briefing (verbatim user words, rollbacks, survivors, queued fixes, concrete anti-patterns) to be/builds/, commit + push, end the session, no continuing. Step 7 is automatic: the next session's DoR reads the WTF state clean. DoD gate applies; optional guardrails mode tightens the successor.
tildes.execNote: Cold (40): grading every diff in the blast radius against intent, and naming concrete anti-patterns, is deep judgment the mechanical tail cannot fake; fan-out justified. Matches tildes.json.

model: fable
slots:
  - id: change_grade    tier: cold   hint: per-diff verdict verified/suspect/broken vs stated intent — read-only, no fixing, fan-out one judgment per commit
  - id: anti_patterns   tier: cold   hint: name the concrete failures ("hallucinated file X"), never vague "be careful" — this text IS the next session's guardrail
schema/data: grade enum {verified, suspect, broken}; rollback menu {revert, fix-forward, accept} — user decides each
---
The AI proposes, the user disposes. Step 6 is terminal: report committed, session ends, no exceptions — trust is rebuilt by the next session's results, not this one's apologies.

tildes.oneLiner: Prepare the project for handoff — scan, confirm-each cleanup, then a self-contained 10-section manifest the recipient opens cold.
tildes.spec: Four forms (bare, to <name>, clean, manifest) over a fixed 9-step chain: a read-only pre-flight scan against pattern tables (secrets, token prefixes, machine paths, build bloat, deps, deploy config), interactive cleanup where nothing is deleted without confirmation, commit + push, then the manifest HTML to be/transfers/ covering overview through machine-specific setup, committed so it travels with the code. Hard invariants: never auto-delete secrets, never touch Be artifacts or board.md, idempotent — twice yields the same result. In Progress items get one question: reset to Ready or hand off mid-work.
tildes.execNote: Warming (70): scan/cleanup/manifest scaffolding is Tier-0, but the Overview + Architecture Notes + Known Issues prose needs synthesis; matches tildes.json — and when a-d-d/ exists the arch slot degrades to a READ, $0.
tildes.pi:
~transfer.pi
---
name: transfer
protocol: [scan_git_board_secrets_paths_deps_deploy, present_findings_by_category, confirm_each_cleanup, ask_in_progress_disposition, commit_push_cleanup, render_manifest_10_sections, update_index, commit_push_manifest, open]
dor: class
dod: class
verb: "transferred from"
model: sonnet
slots:
  - id: overview_arch   tier: warm   hint: Project Overview + Architecture Notes + Known Issues prose; when a-d-d/ exists this becomes a verbatim READ of the spec — Tier 0, $0
schema/data: scan tables (secret globs *.key/*.pem/.env*, token prefixes cfat_/sk-/ghp_, path prefixes C:\ /Users/ /home/) + never-clean list (be/* artifacts, board.md, index.html, CLAUDE.md, deploy.json, source) ride inline
---
The artifact trail is sacred — never delete it, never ask. The manifest is self-contained Be-styled HTML committed into the repo; the handoff travels with the code.

tildes.oneLiner: Render help inline — pre-computed Latest, the byte-verbatim command grid, one unshown quote — no artifact, no commit, ever.
tildes.spec: The only tilde with zero writes: pure display, DoD-exempt, no telemetry. Bare ~h reads be/h-cache.json (pre-filtered, pre-truncated, revision-resolved — render, don't compute), prints the kernel's single-screen grid exactly as written, and closes with one random quote from the session's unshown pool of 34. Subtopics route by table — commands/dod/dor/chain/board to help-blocks.md, any command to commands.md — and the named fenced block displays byte-verbatim, no rewording. metal already extracted the computation into h-cache by hand; nothing is left to think about.
tildes.execNote: 100% Hot (Tier 0): cache render + table route + byte-verbatim block copy + pool-tracked random pick; corrects tildes.json's haiku — even the quote is an RNG over a list, not a judgment.
tildes.pi:
~h.pi
---
name: h
protocol: [route_topic_by_table, read_h_cache, render_latest_3, print_grid_verbatim, pick_random_unshown_quote, or_read_named_section, print_block_byte_verbatim]
dor: exempt
dod: exempt
verb: none
model: none
slots: []
schema/data: routing table {bare → h-cache + kernel grid + quotes.md pool, dod|dor|chain|board|commands → help-blocks.md §, <command> → commands.md §}; quote pool = 34, session-tracked, reset when exhausted
---
Display contract: byte-verbatim or nothing — no paraphrase, no generation. No match prints "no match" and falls back to bare ~h.

tildes.oneLiner: One sweep of the artifact comment threads — execute the operator's tilde-comments, answer the questions, stamp everything seen.
tildes.spec: The operator's on-demand handoff (“I'm done reviewing — act”), deliberately replacing any scheduled poller: zero idle cost, one GET of /comments/recent per invocation. Filters to operator comments (author != metal) not yet seen-by-metal, then dispatches each: a ~-prefixed comment executes as that tilde scoped to its artifact, anything else gets an answer (optionally posted in-thread via POST /comments), and every swept doc is stamped via POST /comments/seen so the receipt flips. Reports per artifact what was found, acted on, and pending. Operates only on the operator-dialogue channel — never institutional-knowledge.
tildes.execNote: Warming (65): the sweep/stamp protocol is Tier-0 and the ~-prefix fast-path is 100% deterministic dispatch; the residual slot is classify-and-light-reply, haiku territory — corrects tildes.json's sonnet (executed tildes rent their own models).
tildes.pi:
~fu.pi
---
name: fu
protocol: [get_comments_recent_one_call, filter_operator_unseen, split_tilde_vs_prose, dispatch_tilde_scoped_to_artifact, post_reply_in_thread, post_seen_per_doc, report_found_acted_pending]
dor: exempt
dod: exempt
verb: "followed up on"
model: haiku
slots:
  - id: comment_classify   tier: warm   hint: only prose comments reach it — the leading ~ is a deterministic fast-path straight to dispatch, $0
  - id: reply_draft        tier: warm   hint: short in-thread answer to a question; anchored by the artifact it hangs on
schema/data: endpoints {GET /comments/recent, POST /comments?doc=&obj= author=metal, POST /comments/seen?doc=} on the gcp-bridge; channel = operator-dialogue only
---
One sweep per invocation, fired by the operator — the zero-idle Attendant. Dispatched tildes run their own .pi cards; ~fu carries only the sweep.

tildes.oneLiner: Dispatch a plain-English browser test to the-last-test cloud — sealed RunResult back, meter fires, repeat runs replay from signed cache for $0.
tildes.spec: The For Rent sibling of local ~t: be/tools/tlt-dispatch.sh POSTs intent + target to api.the-last-test.com/serve/run, billed $0.05/run to the operator's FQDN, and saves the sealed RunResult to be/tests/runs/<run_id>/manifest.json. The pipeline is deterministic machinery — cache keyed SHA-256(intent + target + dom_sig), HMAC-verified before any replay (tampered files exit 5, never execute), a 14-op typed step schema executed by the C# Playwright jacket. The single judgment moment is plan-select on cache miss: intent + DOM snapshot → ordered step list, schema-validated with one retry, then signed and written so the same intent costs zero LLM calls until dom_sig says the page actually drifted.
tildes.execNote: Warming: the entire dispatch/cache/verify/replay chain is Tier-0; the sole slot, plan-select on cache miss, is structured decomposition (intent + live DOM → ordered typed ops), sonnet-class per the band. Corrects tildes.json's haiku — planning steps against a real DOM is not classify/format work; and the slot only rents on a miss, since every hit is $0.
tildes.pi:
~tlt.pi
---
name: tlt
protocol: [read-config(TLT_ENDPOINT, TLT_OPERATOR_FQDN, TLT_RUNS_DIR), build-request(intent, target, operator_fqdn), compute-key(sha256: intent + target + dom_sig), lookup-cache(be/tests/cache/<hash>.json), verify-hmac(operator keystore; BAD_SIG → exit 5, never execute), replay-on-hit($0, llm_called:false), transpile-on-miss(slot: plan_select), validate-steps(14-op schema; reject + retry once), sign-and-write-cache, execute-steps(C# Playwright jacket), seal-RunResult, fire-MeterEvent($0.05/run → operator FQDN), save-manifest(be/tests/runs/<run_id>/manifest.json), print-summary(outcome, duration_ms, record_url)]
dor: exempt          # intent + target ARE the pre-flight; dispatch-only verb
dod: class           # sealed RunResult saved, record_url live, meter fired
verb: "cloud-tested against"
model: sonnet        # rents ONLY on cache miss; every hit is $0
slots:
  - id: plan_select   tier: warm   hint: cache-miss only — intent + target + DOM snapshot → ordered 14-op step list; schema validator + one retry bound it; the run itself is the binary oracle; each signed cache write graduates this intent to hot until dom_sig drifts
schema/data: 14-op step schema (navigate, waitFor, click, fill, type, hover, isVisible, assertText, assertVisible, screenshot, evaluate, close, domSignature, header/meta) · cache key = SHA-256(intent+target+dom_sig) · sig = HMAC-SHA256 keyed off ~/.metal/keystore/<fqdn>.hmac
---
intent → key → hit? verify + replay : transpile → validate → sign → execute → seal → meter. Tampered cache never runs; drifted DOM re-transpiles; the same intent never pays twice.
